title: Diffusion Model Families
description: Families that learn to denoise a representation step by step until it becomes a coherent image, video, audio clip, or other sample.
openingSummary: Diffusion families are easier to recognize by process than by checkpoint name: start from noise or a noisy latent, apply many denoising updates conditioned on a prompt or context, and stop when the sample becomes structured enough to decode back into something a person can use.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A diffusion family bundles models that generate by reversing a corruption process. Instead of predicting only the next token, the model repeatedly cleans up a latent or pixel-space representation. That makes the family especially natural for image and video generation, but the same denoising generation idea also shows up in other modalities.

howItDiffers.title: How It Differs From Nearby Families
howItDiffers.body: Compared with transformer families, the core loop is about iterative denoising rather than walking through a sequence token by token. Compared with a world model, the target is usually a polished sample rather than a persistent internal state that tracks an environment. Compared with multimodal model families, diffusion pages emphasize the generation process itself more than the fusion of different input channels.

representativePatterns.title: Representative Patterns
representativePatterns.body: Readers should look for conditioning paths, latent spaces, and denoising schedules when they inspect a diffusion family. Attention still matters, especially in prompt-conditioned systems, but it appears inside a broader process whose main question is how each denoising step improves the sample.
